segments.text: Oh, that my head were a spring of water, and my eyes a fountain of tears! I would weep day and nightover the slain daughter of my people.
If only I had a traveler’s lodge in the wilderness, I would abandon my people and depart from them, for they are all adulterers, a crowd of faithless people.
They bend their tongues like bows; lies prevail over truth in the land. For they proceed from evil to evil, and they do not take Me into account,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: declares the LORD.
Let everyone guard against his neighbor; do not trust any brother, for every brother deals craftily, and every friend spreads slander.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Each one betrays his friend; no one tells the truth. They have taught their tongues to lie; they wear themselves out committing iniquity.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: You dwell[^19] in the midst of deception; in their deceit they refuse to know Me,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: declares the LORD.
Therefore this is what the LORD of Hosts says:

Behold, I will refine them and test them, for what else can I do
because of[^20] the daughter of My people?
segments.speaker: the Lord of Hosts

segments.text: Their tongues are deadly arrows; they speak deception. With his mouth a man speaks peace to his neighbor, but in his heart he sets a trap for him.
segments.speaker: the Lord of Hosts

segments.text: Should I not punish them for these things?declares the LORD. Should I not avenge Myselfon such a nation as this?
segments.speaker: the Lord of Hosts

segments.text: I will take up a weeping and wailing for the mountains, a dirge over the wilderness pasture, for they have been scorched so no one passes through, and the lowing of cattle is not heard. Both the birds of the air and the beasts have fled; they have gone away.
And I will make Jerusalem a heap of rubble, a haunt for jackals; [^21] and I will make the cities of Judah a desolation, without inhabitant.
segments.speaker: the Lord of Hosts

segments.text: Who is the man wise enough to understand this? To whom has the mouth of the LORD spoken, that he may explain it? Why is the land destroyed and scorched like a desert, so no one can pass through it?
And the LORD answered,
It is because they have forsaken My law, which I set before them; they have not walked in it or obeyed My voice.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Instead, they have followed the stubbornness of their hearts and gone after the Baals, as their fathers taught them.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Therefore this is what the LORD of Hosts, the God of Israel, says:
Behold, I will feed this people wormwood and give them poisoned water to drink.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: I will scatter them among the nations that neither they nor their fathers have known, and I will send a sword after them until I have finished them off.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: This is what the LORD of Hosts says:
Take note, and summon the wailing women; send for the most skillful among them.
segments.speaker: the Lord of Hosts

segments.text: Let them come quickly and take up a lament over us, that our eyes may overflow with tears, and our eyelids may gush with water.
segments.speaker: the Lord of Hosts

segments.text: For the sound of wailingis heard from Zion:‘How devastated we are! How great is our shame! For we have abandoned the landbecause our dwellings have been torn down.’
segments.speaker: the Lord of Hosts

segments.text: Now, O women, hear the word of the LORD. Open your ears to the word of His mouth. Teach your daughters to wail, and one another to lament.
For death has climbed in through our windows; it has entered our fortressesto cut off the children from the streets, the young men from the town squares.
Declare that this is what the LORD says:
The corpses of men will fall like dungupon the open field, like newly cut grain behind the reaper, with no one to gather it.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: This is what the LORD says:

Let not the wise man boast in his wisdom, nor the strong man in his strength, 
nor the wealthy man in his riches.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: But let him who boasts boast in this, that he understands and knows Me, that I am the LORD, [^22] who exercises loving devotion, [^23] justice and righteousness on the earth—for I delight in these things,
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: declares the LORD.
Behold, the days are coming,
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: declares the LORD,
when I will punish all who are circumcised yet uncircumcised:
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Egypt, Judah, Edom, Ammon, Moab, and all the inhabitants of the desert who clip the hair of their temples. For all these nations are uncircumcised, and the whole house of Israel is uncircumcised in heart.
segments.speaker: the Lord
